title: To provide that the final rule of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service titled "Endangered and Threatened Wildlife and Plants; Endangered Species Status With Critical Habitat for Guadalupe Fatmucket, Texas Fatmucket, Guadalupe Orb, Texas Pimpleback, Balcones Spike, and False Spike, and Threatened Species Status With Section 4(d) Rule and Critical Habitat for Texas Fawnsfoot" shall have no force or effect.
plain_summary: This bill would cancel a government rule that protects seven types of freshwater mussels that live in Texas rivers and streams. The rule had listed these mussels as endangered or threatened species, which means they were in danger of disappearing forever. The rule also set aside special areas where these mussels live that had to be protected from harm. If this bill becomes law, those protections would go away and the mussels would no longer be protected under the Endangered Species Act.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1  Rule Relating to Endangered Species and Threatened Species Status of Certain Species of Mussels
sections.text: The final rule created by the United States Fish and Wildlife Service is canceled and will not be enforced. This rule was called Endangered and Threatened Wildlife and Plants and it gave endangered species protection to six types of freshwater mussels called Guadalupe Fatmucket, Texas Fatmucket, Guadalupe Orb, Texas Pimpleback, Balcones Spike, and False Spike. The rule also gave threatened species protection to another mussel called Texas Fawnsfoot and created special habitat areas that must be protected for all these mussels. This rule was published in the Federal Register on June fourth, two thousand twenty four. None of this rule can be used or enforced anymore.
